claims.evidence: rng.js line 4: `window.SI = window.SI || {};` runs once (rng.js loads first per MODULE_ORDER); collision.js, config.js, loop.js each attach via `window.SI.X = {...}` without re-declaring or re-initializing window.SI.

rng.js implements mulberry32: seed(n) sets `state = n >>> 0`; next() advances state and returns `(t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296` which is in [0,1) since numerator max is 2^32-1; same seed -> same state sequence -> reproducible; different seed values produce different state, diverging output.

collision.js aabbOverlap uses strict `<` in all four axis comparisons (`a.x < b.x + b.w && b.x < a.x + a.w && a.y < b.y + b.h && b.y < a.y + a.h`), so real overlap/containment -> true, separated boxes -> false, and edge-touch (e.g. a.x + a.w === b.x) fails the strict `<` -> false.

config.js: FIXED_TIMESTEP_MS = 1000/60 (≈16.667, positive), ALIEN_ROWS:5, ALIEN_COLS:11 (5x11 grid), STARTING_LIVES:3, ALIEN_POINTS_ROW_LOW/MID/HIGH = 10/20/30, UFO_BONUS_MIN:50, UFO_BONUS_MAX:300 — all exact values match intent.

loop.js frame(): accumulator += delta then `while (accumulator >= step) { SI.Game.update(step); accumulator -= step; }` gives integer update() calls with remainder carried in accumulator across frames; `var cap = step * 3; if (delta > cap) delta = cap;` caps huge deltas (spiral-of-death guard); SI.Renderer.draw() is called exactly once per frame() invocation, outside the while loop, before requesting the next rAF frame.

build.js MODULE_ORDER = ['rng.js','collision.js','config.js','loop.js'] concatenated via fs.readFileSync/path.join (only 'fs' and 'path' required, ADR-004) into dist/game.js via fs.writeFileSync; buildHtml() wraps the same bundle string with a <canvas id="game"> element, minimal CSS, and a window.gameState stub literal with state:'ready' (plus score/lives/wave/fps/player/aliens/bullets/shields/ufo fields per ADR-003) into dist/index.html; main() always fs.writeFileSync's both outputs unconditionally from the same deterministic inputs, so re-running produces byte-identical output (idempotent).

claims.text: rng.js defaults to seed(1) at module load time so SI.RNG.next() is deterministic even if no caller ever calls seed() explicitly, beyond the bare 'seedable' requirement.
claims.evidence: rng.js line 25: `seed(1);` called inside the IIFE before exposing window.SI.RNG

claims.text: Each module is wrapped in its own IIFE to keep internal state (RNG's `state` var, Loop's `accumulator`/`lastTime`/`running`) private and non-global, beyond what the intent claims required.
claims.evidence: rng.js and loop.js both wrap module bodies in `(function () { ... })();`

claims.text: SI.Loop exposes a stop() control (running=false) in addition to start(), giving callers a way to halt the rAF loop cleanly, which the intent claims did not explicitly request.
claims.evidence: loop.js: `function stop() { running = false; }` exported as SI.Loop.stop

claims.text: dist/index.html's gameState stub includes extra fields beyond a bare 'ready' flag — score, lives, wave, fps, player rect, aliens/bullets/shields arrays, and a ufo object — anticipating later slices' state shape.
